title: pediomelum castoreum
intro: Pediomelum castoreum, also known as castor bean or mole plant, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its yellow or white flowers and distinctive seed pods containing the toxic and highly valued ricin. The seeds of the pediomelum castoreum plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including pain, fever, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of pediomelum castoreum are believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the seeds of the pediomelum castoreum plant are typically extracted and used in tinctures, salves, or infusions, although caution is advised due to the toxicity of the ricin.
